articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google announces Gemma 4 open AI models with improved capabilities and switches to Apache 2.0 license. This represents a significant update to Google's open model offerings after a year.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduces two new inference tiers, Flex and Priority, to the Gemini API for better cost-latency optimization. This gives developers more granular control over performance trade-offs.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids receives AI capabilities powered by Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1 models, including high-quality video generation at no cost. The update brings advanced video creation tools to Google Workspace.

articles.title: Microsoft takes on AI rivals with three new foundational models
articles.summary: Microsoft's MAI group releases new models for voice transcription, audio generation, and image creation. This represents Microsoft's effort to compete more directly with other AI model providers.

articles.title: llm-gemini 0.30
articles.summary: Update to the LLM plugin for accessing Google's Gemini models. This tool update likely includes compatibility improvements and new features.

articles.title: Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Survey on Agentic RAG
articles.summary: Comprehensive survey on agentic RAG systems that combine LLMs with autonomous data retrieval capabilities. The paper analyzes how these systems can provide more contextually relevant and dynamic responses compared to traditional RAG approaches.

articles.title: Decision-Centric Design for LLM Systems
articles.summary: Proposes separating decision-relevant signals from the policy that maps them to actions in LLM systems. This framework aims to make control decisions more explicit and inspectable rather than entangled within generation.

articles.title: Ontology-Constrained Neural Reasoning in Enterprise Agentic Systems
articles.summary: Presents a neurosymbolic architecture that addresses enterprise LLM limitations through ontology-constrained reasoning. The approach introduces structured frameworks for role, domain, and interaction ontologies to reduce hallucination and ensure regulatory compliance.

articles.title: Making Sense of AI Agents Hype: Adoption, Architectures, and Takeaways from Practitioners
articles.summary: Comprehensive review of 138 practitioner conference talks on AI agents, analyzing real-world adoption patterns and architectural strategies. The study provides insights into how companies are actually implementing agent-based systems in production environments.

articles.title: The Silicon Mirror: Dynamic Behavioral Gating for Anti-Sycophancy in LLM Agents
articles.summary: Introduces a framework to detect and counter sycophantic behavior in LLMs through dynamic behavioral access control. The system adjusts AI behavior in real-time to maintain factual integrity when users employ persuasion tactics.

articles.title: OpenAI acquires TBPN, the buzzy founder-led business talk show
articles.summary: OpenAI acquires TBPN, Silicon Valley's popular tech podcast, which will operate independently under Chris Lehane's oversight. This represents OpenAI's expansion into media and content creation beyond AI model development.

articles.title: Chatbots are now prescribing psychiatric drugs
articles.summary: Utah allows an AI system to prescribe psychiatric drugs without a doctor in a one-year pilot program. The initiative aims to reduce costs and address care shortages, but physicians raise concerns about transparency and safety.

articles.title: Perplexity's 'Incognito Mode' is a 'sham,' lawsuit says
articles.summary: Lawsuit alleges that Google, Meta, and Perplexity share millions of user chats to increase ad revenue, despite privacy claims. The case highlights concerns about data practices in AI chat services.

articles.title: PSA: Anyone with a link can view your Granola notes by default
articles.summary: The AI note-taking app Granola makes notes viewable to anyone with a link by default and uses them for AI training unless users opt out. This raises privacy concerns for users who may not realize their meeting notes are accessible.

articles.title: Highlights from my conversation about agentic engineering on Lenny's Podcast
articles.summary: Simon Willison discusses agentic engineering concepts and practices on Lenny's Podcast. The conversation likely covers practical aspects of building AI agent systems and current industry trends.

articles.title: The Axios supply chain attack used individually targeted social engineering
articles.summary: Analysis of a supply chain attack against Axios that employed targeted social engineering tactics. This highlights the evolving sophistication of attacks against software development infrastructure.
